No, yeah, they're not joining today.
We can get started.
Okay, cool.

So, despite the drop, the positions didn't really change.
They're still higher compared to two weeks ago.
So, generally, things are looking up over there.
For the per diem pages, there were no major changes, really.
I did notice that the number of .gov websites showing up seems to have increased a lot, especially in the more populous states.

Just one question real quick.
Thank guys.
Thank you, Thank you, guys.
you, guys.

These are the ones that we created.
Okay.
You either created or you updated or...

She was working with Abby, I think, and they created some new pages or they updated some of the blogs, and that's what this is.
Got it.
Okay.
Do we know which one, if any specific one caused this spike?
Yeah, I had listed them last week.
So it was basically these ones that had the highest job service cash budget.
Um...
Cache flow statement indirect method, and basically these ones over here.
Okay.

I think they were.

Okay, cool.
Yeah, just clarifying.

So, yeah, the per-dm page is basically more .com showing up, but, you know, we seem to be holding position reasonably well compared to Travel Perk and Navan, which are the only competitors there.
For the expense category, slight improvement in performance the past three weeks.
It's pretty much the same.

For the tuning pages, pretty much the same performance as last week.
No major sort of.
Changes here.

With these few pages here.
For the vendor pages, slight high by the looks of the chart, but then the review, I think, to review pages increased as it did to versus pages.
Alternative pages fell as a group, and that's why we didn't make a new high, basically.
Amongst the categories of pages, project management and DevOps are the biggest gains, and CRM and AI infrastructure saw the biggest drops.
Generally, the AI pages' average position improved, and they're in the top 10, so it's a pretty good performance for the AI pages.
Generally, they've been far and away the best performers in this group of pages so far.
Regarding content refreshes, again, we only have like four pages in here, no major changes observed.
So, yeah, that's pretty much that.
Is there anything I can answer about the vendor pages or dive deeper into some of this?
No, that all makes sense, and it's helpful to know, too, like, that the review pages increased and alternative pages didn't.
I'm curious, like, how are you tracking performance internally for these pages?
Like, are you using any, like, are you using tools like Amplitude or something, or is it just GF or GSE?
Yeah, we have a Looker dashboard tracking.
Thank you.
Then, so user count, user sessions, MQLs, SQLs, so yeah, but we don't, we don't have it broken down by page type.
We were able to see by page specifically, but not aggregated by the type of page, so.
Okay, so it's not clustered together, basically.
No, yeah, I mean, we can, yeah, now that we have this, this is helpful, yeah.
The question about the MQL, SQL, like I'm assuming you're integrating to HubSpot or your CRM and then qualifying it that way, or is it some other definition you're using for that?
Yeah, use multi-touch and it's just, yeah, coming from, from the touch points.
Exactly.
Cool.
Salesforce, yeah, it's just integrated with Looker.
Okay, yeah, I was asking because I was just wondering, like, is there anything else we can add to our Looker to sort of give you that bigger visibility?
But, okay.
Yeah, this is really helpful, like, knowing by the posters.
Okay, and yeah, if there's anything else you want us to track, not just in vendor pages, but, you know, any of the other categories, do let us know, like, we can build it out for sure.
Seems good.
So, following on from last week, I know you saw the thread I just sent over the vendor categories with the tool lists.
Let me know what you think about that.
And also, I shared the template and the sample article for best CRM for, I think it was startups that I sent over.
So, yeah, let me know what you think about it, any changes, et cetera, and if it's good, you know, we can start scaling those.
Sounds good.
Yeah, thank you for sending those over.
Yeah, the template looks good.
The only thing, what I'm thinking is this would be a new page, right?
So how, what would be the best way to integrate with the current hierarchy and like with the pages architecture?
The UX generally.
We can, there's two ways of doing this.
One is like, since we already have a CRM category, we can definitely, I'm trying to organize my thoughts, sorry.
We can have, we can append like one section in the existing CRM, whatever articles in the individual vendor articles and link to the new article.
And the new article itself, we can have a separate section on the main vendor landing page that just says, you know, right now, so we.
Have the tool categories on the left hand side.
We can have a separate submenu in there saying for startups, for whatever.
And then, you know, it can be like a separate section on that page.
So it will be like for startups, enterprise, and then CRM or other categories of tools.
Like, I don't know.
I know we're not doing ERP, but example ERP or, you know, CMS and so on.
So we could do it that way.
And I think that would be the simplest, even though it'll have a large footprint.
That might be the best way in terms of just pure UX to get it done.
Because then you'll probably find it easier to nest it as well in your backend CMS in this structure.
So that's how I was thinking.
Makes sense.
just to make sure I understand.
So on the existing pages, just add sections for each one of the segments.
And I'll just link out to this page, but not include, so you wouldn't include on the main menu, right?
Well, let me link to this.
Let's look at the page itself.
I don't think we should link on the main menu, but at the same time, like for discoverability.
Okay.
So when you say main menu, you're saying like, you don't want it showing up over here.
Correct.
Yeah.
Okay.
Yeah.
They could be hidden.
I mean, what is the play here?
It's purely SEO, right?
Like you're not trying to showcase your data, obviously.
If that's the case, then yeah, we could probably interlink from the existing vendor pages and publish them.
So they won't show up on this page, but then, you know, they'll live somewhere on the website.
That could be one way of doing it.
We could initially do it that way and see if it works.
Then if the pages are doing well, maybe create a separate section for them.
Makes sense.
And how about the URL structure?
They would probably live vendors slash you'll need a new subfolder for sure.
I think we'll need a separate subfolder for like...
Startup.
Let me think about that and I'll get back to you because I think I have a lot of ideas pinging in my head and I might just end up confusing everything.
So I will...
It's good to talk about it.
As you're sharing, I'm also thinking one way to do it would be to have separate...
So folders for each segment, and then list the best tools for those segments, or just keep growing from the existing from the vendors.
I mean, I think growing from existing will become super unwieldy, like it's just going to expand massively.
Like, for example, if we're doing CRM, right, so if I click any of the CRM, so we're doing vendors, categories, CRM.
And then we need like subfolders under this, so slash, I don't know, business maturity, slash startup, mid-market, enterprise.
Like, I don't know if that folder structure is very manageable.
Like, it might just be a little bit.
Yeah, definitely too long.
But this is the category page, right?
It would be linked under, if you click in one of the tools here, maybe close review.
Yeah.
Okay, goes back to vendors.
Yeah, like my thought is we could expand from here.
But yeah, let's think about it.
Yeah, I don't know if we can expand from individual vendors because like we might not, close might not show up, for example, in the recommended stage for startup.
It might show up for enterprise, but not for startup or something like that.
So yeah, I don't know if linking from or if structuring it under the individual vendors themselves makes sense.
But let me think about it.
I'll send over a proposed folder structure for that.
Okay.
Yeah.
And then on, on, on.
The other point about the new vendors for the list you guys sent, I think we can go ahead and start creating the content for them, and we will just not include the data section for now.
I highlighted a few that we can start with, I think three.

And I know we have data for a couple of the vendors within those categories, so maybe we can, the challenging part is the average, like since we only have two, or I think we can really get the data, but yeah, regardless.
Sorry, go on.
Yeah.
was just going to say regardless, we can create the content.
Yeah.
And I also want to ask like how the page.
This is versus the existing ones.
Yeah, data versus no data.
I remember from last week, we decided we'd be following the same structure, except we just won't have graphs.
So that is still fine.
My guess is we're going to need some images.
It's just going to be like a block of text otherwise, but let's see how it goes.
Yeah, let's start with one category, just so we can keep things like feedback loop manageable.
Yeah, yeah, we'll get on that and we'll start producing some of those ones you earmarked from tomorrow.
And I think we should be able to have at least a few published by this time next week.
So, yeah.
Okay, yeah, I'll be out of office next week.
Thank you.
you.
you.
So, and I would like to review them as they get published.
So if we can't get them up this week, maybe let's hold, or I'll just set with you.
We can keep a few in the pipeline.
Like, we won't publish them, but we'll just, like, stage them or something, and then when you're back, you can take a look and, you know.
Okay, sounds good.
Yeah, we'll do maybe, like, three or four of those just to give you an idea of what it looks like, and then you can scale beyond that when you're back.
Perfect.
Okay, sounds good.
So I will get back to you before, are you leaving?
You're here until Friday, right?
So I can, okay, cool.
So I will get you the folder structure ideas by Friday, and then, yeah, we'll begin sort of creating the sample once after that.
now.
Okay, okay.
You

Cool.
Are you going on vacation or?

Oh, cool.
Nice.
Well, hope you enjoy yourself.
Thank you.
All right.
So yeah, cool.
That was pretty much it, Victoria.

Sounds great.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly performance update and content strategy discussion for RAMP's vendor pages and blog content.](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=12.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Blog pages saw overall improvement despite recent drops; per diem pages stable with increased .gov competition](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=17.0)
  - [Vendor pages showing mixed performance across categories; AI pages performing exceptionally well](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=240.0)
  - [New "best tools for startups" content strategy discussed; URL structure and integration with existing pages to be finalized](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=396.0)
  - [Victoria will be out of office next week; content creation to continue but publishing on hold for review](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=894.0)

## Topics

### SEO Performance Update

  - [Blog pages: Boost in performance over past 2-3 weeks, positions higher than two weeks ago](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=17.0)
  - [Per diem pages: Stable, with increased .gov website competition in populous states](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=38.0)
  - [Expense category: Slight improvement over past three weeks](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=162.0)
  - [Tuning pages: Performance consistent with previous week](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=172.0)
  - [Treasury pages: Holding steady pattern](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=203.0)
  - [Vendor pages:](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=149.0)
      - [Slight overall increase](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=212.0)
      - [Review and versus pages increased](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=212.0)
      - [Alternative pages decreased](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=225.0)
      - [Project management and DevOps saw biggest gains](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=231.0)
      - [CRM and AI infrastructure experienced largest drops](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=231.0)
      - [AI pages showing best performance, improved average position in top 10](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=240.0)

### Content Strategy for New "Best Tools" Pages

  - [Template for "best CRM for startups" shared and approved](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=407.0)
  - [Discussion on integration with current page hierarchy and UX:](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=448.0)
      - [Proposal to add sections in existing pages linking to new content](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=472.0)
      - [Consideration of separate submenu on main vendor landing page](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)
      - [Agreement not to include in main navigation menu](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=566.0)
  - [URL structure to be determined; possible new subfolder for startup-focused content](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=618.0)
  - [Victoria highlighted 3 categories to start with for new vendor content](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=796.0)
  - [Content creation to proceed without data section for now](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=780.0)
  - [Plan to create 3-4 sample pages for review when Victoria returns](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=920.0)

### Internal Performance Tracking

  - [RAMP uses Looker dashboard to track metrics (user count, sessions, MQLs, SQLs)](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=295.0)
  - [Current tracking is by individual page, not aggregated by page type](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
  - [Multi-touch attribution model integrated with Salesforce](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=349.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Vivek to propose folder structure for new "best tools" content by Friday](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=772.0)
  - [Begin creating sample content for 3-4 new vendor pages (without publishing)](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=878.0)
  - [Continue content creation for new vendor categories, excluding data section](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=780.0)
  - [Victoria to review new content upon return from vacation](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=900.0)
  - [Vivek to follow up on Slack with any urgent matters during Victoria's absence](https://fathom.video/share/tBoVx7Y3CBf5xG8iSks_Lccm9YxfzWhn?tab=summary&timestamp=986.0)

action_items.description: Send proposed folder structure for new vendor pages by Friday
Begin content creation for new vendor categories w/o data sections, focusing on highlighted ones
Create 3-4 sample vendor pages w/o data sections for Victoria's review. Use highlighted categories.
